skill.name: social-media-finder-skill
skill.description: Automatically find social media profiles across multiple platforms using the BrowserAct API. Supports searching by name for profiles on Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, LinkedIn, and more.
skill.summary: Finds social media profiles for any person or brand using the BrowserAct API and returns structured results including profile URLs and follower counts.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: After evaluating all 50 static findings in context, every finding is a false positive. The skill is a legitimate wrapper around the BrowserAct API for social media profile discovery. Network calls target a single documented API endpoint (api.browseract.com). API key handling follows the standard pattern of reading from environment variables. External commands flagged in SKILL.md are markdown documentation blocks instructing the AI agent to run the Python script, not active code execution. The heuristic critical finding (code execution + network + credential access) is expected behavior for any skill that calls a third-party API with an API key. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious patterns were detected.

content.value_statement: Manually searching for social media profiles across multiple platforms is time-consuming and inefficient. This skill automates the process using the BrowserAct API to find profiles for individuals, brands, and businesses across Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, LinkedIn, and more.
actual_capabilities: Find social media profiles for any person or brand name across multiple platforms
Retrieve profile URLs, follower counts, bio snippets, and page titles
Export results as a downloadable CSV file for offline analysis
Handle name formatting with plus-separated or space-separated input
Provide clear error messages for invalid API keys or expired credentials
Automatically retry once on transient task failures
limitations: Requires a paid BrowserAct API key to function
Only searches by name, not by email, phone number, or username
Results depend on the quality and completeness of third-party API data
Each search can take several minutes to complete

use_cases.title: Sales Lead Profile Research
use_cases.description: Find social media profiles of sales prospects to personalize outreach and build rapport.
use_cases.target_user: Sales professionals and business development representatives

use_cases.title: Candidate Background Check
use_cases.description: Research the professional online presence of job candidates across LinkedIn, Twitter, and other platforms.

use_cases.title: Brand Social Media Audit
use_cases.description: Audit a brand or competitor social media footprint to understand their multi-platform presence.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing managers and competitive intelligence analysts

prompt_templates.title: Basic Profile Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find social media profiles for John Smith.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to quickly find all social media accounts for a specific person

prompt_templates.title: Search with Name Formatting
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all social media profiles for the brand Tesla. Use plus signs between words for better results.
prompt_templates.scenario: User is searching for a brand or multi-word name and wants optimal formatting

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Intelligence
prompt_templates.prompt: Search social media for each of our top three competitors: CompanyA, CompanyB, and CompanyC. Give me a summary of their follower counts on each platform.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to compare social media presence across multiple competitors

prompt_templates.title: Lead Generation Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a list of 20 prospects from my CRM. Find each person social media profiles and compile the results into a single report with profile URLs and follower counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to enrich CRM data with social media profiles in bulk

output_examples.input: Find social media profiles for Elon Musk
output_examples.output: Results include LinkedIn profile with 15M+ followers and bio about Tesla and SpaceX, Twitter/X profile with 150M+ followers, and Instagram account with 45M+ followers. Each entry includes the profile URL, platform name, follower count, and bio snippet.

output_examples.input: Search for the social media presence of Nike
output_examples.output: Brand profiles found: Instagram with 300M+ followers, Twitter/X with 8M+ followers, Facebook with 35M+ followers, LinkedIn company page with 10M+ followers, and TikTok with 5M+ followers. All profile URLs and follower counts are included.

best_practices: Use plus signs between first and last names for better search accuracy (e.g., John+Smith)
Ensure the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable is set before running the skill
Monitor timestamped status logs in the terminal output to track task progress
anti_patterns: Do not retry when the error message indicates an invalid or expired API key
Do not search for multiple unrelated names in a single task execution
Do not interrupt the script while it is polling for task completion

faq.question: What API key do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API key. Get one by visiting the BrowserAct Console and set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: How long does each social media search take?
faq.answer: Each search can take several minutes because the API performs automated browser operations across multiple platforms. The script outputs timestamped status logs so you can monitor progress.

faq.question: Which social media platforms are supported?
faq.answer: The skill searches across Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, and other major platforms. Results include profile URLs, follower counts, and bio snippets.

faq.question: Can I search for profiles using an email address?
faq.answer: No, this skill only supports searching by name. It cannot search by email address, phone number, or username.

faq.question: What happens if the task fails during execution?
faq.answer: If the error is a transient failure like a network issue, the skill automatically retries once. If the error indicates an invalid API key, it will not retry and will ask you to provide a valid key.

faq.question: Is my API key and search data secure?
faq.answer: Your API key is read from the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable and sent securely to the BrowserAct API. Results are returned to your terminal and are not stored by this skill.
